info.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM - Questionnaire Templates
info.description:
Last Updated January 2026

You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM.

Whether you're experienced with or new to REST, use this guide to find what you need, including:

  • A quick start how-to that walks you through a simple request example.
  • Use cases that provide real-world solutions with code examples.
  • Detailed descriptions for each REST resource.
info.x-summary: Last Updated January 2026 You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM.

parameters.name: questionnaireTemplatesUniqID
parameters.description: This is the hash key of the attributes which make up the composite key for the Questionnaire Templates resource and used to uniquely identify an instance of Questionnaire Templates. The client should not generate the hash key value. Instead, the client should query on the Questionnaire Templates collection resource in order to navigate to a specific instance of Questionnaire Templates to get the hash key.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: When this parameter is provided, the specified children are included in the resource payload (instead of just a link). The value of this query parameter is "all" or "". More than one child can be specified using comma as a separator. Example: ?expand=Employees,Localizations. Nested children can also be provided following the format "Child.NestedChild" (Example: ?expand=Employees.Managers). If a nested child is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?expand=Employees.Managers" is the same as "?expand=Employees,Employees.Managers" (which will expand Employees and Managers).

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
This parameter filters the resource fields. Only the specified fields are returned, which means that if no fields are specified, no fields are returned (useful to get only the links). If an indirect child resource is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?fields=Employees.Managers:Empname" is the same as "?fields=;Employees:;Employees.Managers:Empname" (which will only return the "Empname" field for Managers). the value of this query parameter is a list of resource fields. The attribute can be a direct (Example: Employees) or indirect (Example: Employees.Managers) child. It cannot be combined with expand query parameter. If both are provided, only fields will be considered.

Format: ?fields=Attribute1,Attribute2

Format for fields in child resource: ?fields=Accessor1:Attribute1,Attribute2

parameters.name: onlyData
parameters.description: The resource item payload will be filtered in order to contain only data (no links section, for example).

parameters.name: dependency
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies dependencies which are fields that are set before and rolled back after generating the response. Generally they are used to preview the effects of an attribute change. The fields specified in this parameter are always set in the resource instance in question. When a child resource collection is requested and the parameter is set, the fields will be set in the parent resource instance before generating the resource collection payload. The value of this query parameter is a set of dependency fields. Example: dependency=ProductId=2

Format: <attr1>=<val1>,<attr2>=<value2>

parameters.name: links
parameters.description:
This parameter can be used to show only certain links while accessing a singular resource or a resource collection. The parameter value format is a comma-separated list of : <link_relation>

Example:
self,canonical

parameters.name: REST-Framework-Version
parameters.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.

parameters.name: Metadata-Context
parameters.description: If the REST API supports runtime customizations, the shape of the service may change during runtime. The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

parameters.name: Effective-Of
parameters.description: This header accepts a string value. The string is a semi-colon separated list of <parameter-name>=<parameter-value>. It is used to perform effective date range operations. The accepted parameters are RangeMode, RangeSpan, RangeStartDate, RangeEndDate, RangeStartSequence and RangeEndSequence. The parameter values are always strings. The possible values for RangeMode are SET_LOGICAL_START, SET_LOGICAL_END, END_DATE, SET_EFFECTIVE_START, SET_EFFECTIVE_END, REPLACE_CORRECTION, REPLACE_UPDATE, RECONCILE_CORRECTION, CORRECTION, RECONCILE_UPDATE, UPDATE, ZAP and DELETE_CHANGES. The possible values for RangeSpan are PHYSICAL_ROW_END_DATE and LOGICAL_ROW_END_DATE. The values for RangeStartDate and RangeEndDate have to be a string representation of a date in yyyy-MM-dd format. The value for RangeStartSequence and RangeEndSequence must be strings such that when parsed they yield positive integers.

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.

attachments.description: The attachments resource is used to view, create, and update attachments.

SubscriberLov.description:
The following properties are defined on the LOV link relation:
  • Source Attribute: SubscriberCode; Target Attribute: SubscriberCode
  • Display Attribute: SubscriberCode

sections.description: The sections resource is a child resource of the questionnaireTemplates resource that includes the details of the sections defined in the questionnaire template.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: When this parameter is provided, the specified children are included in the resource payload (instead of just a link). The value of this query parameter is "all" or "". More than one child can be specified using comma as a separator. Example: ?expand=Employees,Localizations. Nested children can also be provided following the format "Child.NestedChild" (Example: ?expand=Employees.Managers). If a nested child is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?expand=Employees.Managers" is the same as "?expand=Employees,Employees.Managers" (which will expand Employees and Managers).

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
This parameter filters the resource fields. Only the specified fields are returned, which means that if no fields are specified, no fields are returned (useful to get only the links). If an indirect child resource is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?fields=Employees.Managers:Empname" is the same as "?fields=;Employees:;Employees.Managers:Empname" (which will only return the "Empname" field for Managers). the value of this query parameter is a list of resource fields. The attribute can be a direct (Example: Employees) or indirect (Example: Employees.Managers) child. It cannot be combined with expand query parameter. If both are provided, only fields will be considered.

Format: ?fields=Attribute1,Attribute2

Format for fields in child resource: ?fields=Accessor1:Attribute1,Attribute2

parameters.name: onlyData
parameters.description: The resource item payload will be filtered in order to contain only data (no links section, for example).

parameters.name: links
parameters.description:
This parameter can be used to show only certain links while accessing a singular resource or a resource collection. The parameter value format is a comma-separated list of : <link_relation>

Example:
self,canonical

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: This parameter restricts the number of resources returned inside the resource collection. If the limit exceeds the resource count then the framework will only return the available resources.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Used to define the starting position of the resource collection. If offset exceeds the resource count then no resources are returned. Default value is 0.

parameters.name: totalResults
parameters.description: The resource collection representation will include the "estimated row count" when "?totalResults=true", otherwise the count is not included.  The default value is "false".

parameters.name: q
parameters.description:
This query parameter defines the where clause. The resource collection will be queried using the provided expressions. The value of this query parameter is one or more expressions. Example: ?q=Deptno>=10 and <= 30;Loc!=NY

Format: ?q=expression1;expression2

You can use these queryable attributes to filter this collection resource using the q query parameter:
  • CreatedBy; string; Name of the user who created the questionnaire template.
  • CreationDate; string; Date when the questionnaire template was created.
  • Editor; string; Person Identifier of the person having the privileges to edit the questionnaire template marked as private.
  • EditorPersonName; string; Name of the person with privileges to edit the questionnaire template marked as private.
  • EditorPersonNumber; string; Person number of the person with privileges to edit the questionnaire template that's marked private.
  • InUseFlag; boolean; Indicates whether the questionnaire template is used by any questionnaires. The default value is false.
  • InUseMeaning; string; Meaning of the In Use Code attribute that Indicates whether the questionnaire template is used by any questionnaire.
  • InstructionText; string; Instructions or additional information for the questionnaire template.
  • LastUpdateDate; string; Date when the questionnaire template was last updated.
  • LastUpdatedBy; string; Name of the user who last updated the questionnaire template.
  • PrivacyFlag; boolean; Indicates whether the questionnaire template is private. If a template is marked private, only the person specified by the owner attribute can edit the template. The default value is false.
  • PrivacyMeaning; string; Meaning of the privacy code indicating if the questionnaire template is marked private.
  • QuestionnaireTemplateId; integer; Surrogate identifier for the questionnaire template.
  • QuestionnaireTemplateName; string; Name of the questionnaire template.
  • ScoreQuestionnaireFlag; boolean; Indicates whether the questionnaire is scored. The default value is false.
  • Status; string; Status of the questionnaire template.
  • StatusMeaning; string; Meaning of the status code for the questionnaire template.
  • SubscriberCode; string; Code for the subscriber using the questionnaire template. Valid values are defined in the list questionnaireSubscribersLOV.
  • SubscriberId; integer; Surrogate identifier for the subscriber.
  • SubscriberName; string; Name of the subscriber using the questionnaire template.
  • VersionNumber; integer; Version number of the questionnaire template.

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description: This parameter orders a resource collection based on the specified fields.  The parameter value is a comma-separated string of attribute names, each optionally followed by a colon and "asc" or "desc".  Specify "asc" for ascending and "desc" for descending. The default value is "asc".  For example, ?orderBy=field1:asc,field2:desc

parameters.name: finder
parameters.description:
Used as a predefined finder to search the collection.

Format ?finder=<finderName>;<variableName>=<variableValue>,<variableName2>=<variableValue2>

The following are the available finder names and corresponding finder variables

  • PrimaryKey Finds a questionnaire template that matches the specified primary key criteria.
    Finder Variables
    • QuestionnaireTemplateId; integer; Surrogate identifier for the questionnaire template.

x-adf-finder-formats.name: PrimaryKey
x-adf-finder-formats.description: Finds a questionnaire template that matches the specified primary key criteria.

QuestionnaireTemplateId.description: Surrogate identifier for the questionnaire template.
QuestionnaireTemplateId.title: Questionnaire Template Id

parameters.name: effectiveDate
parameters.description: This query parameter is used to fetch resources which are effective dated as of the specified start date. The value of this query parameter is a string having a date in yyyy-MM-dd format

parameters.name: REST-Framework-Version
parameters.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.

parameters.name: Metadata-Context
parameters.description: If the REST API supports runtime customizations, the shape of the service may change during runtime. The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

The possible values for RangeSpan are PHYSICAL_ROW_END_DATE and LOGICAL_ROW_END_DATE. The values for RangeStartDate and RangeEndDate have to be a string representation of a date in yyyy-MM-dd format. The value for RangeStartSequence and RangeEndSequence must be strings such that when parsed they yield positive integers.

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.

AllowFormatChangeFlag.title: Allow changes to format options
AllowFormatChangeFlag.description: Indicates whether the format options can be changed in the questionnaire. The default value is false.

AllowInstrChangeFlag.title: Allow changes to instructions
AllowInstrChangeFlag.description: Indicates whether the instructions can be changed in the questionnaire. The default value is false.

AllowPlainTextFlag.title: Plain Text Box
AllowPlainTextFlag.description: Indicates whether questions with plain text response type can be added to the questionnaire. The default value is false.

AllowRichTextFlag.title: Rich Text Box
AllowRichTextFlag.description: Indicates whether questions with rich text response type can be added to the questionnaire. The default value is false.

AllowSectionChangeFlag.title: Allow changes to sections
AllowSectionChangeFlag.description: Indicates whether a section can be changed in the questionnaire. The default value is false.

CalculationRule.title: Score Calculation Rule Code
CalculationRule.description: Calculation rule code for questionnaires that are scored.

CalculationRuleMeaning.title: Score Calculation Rule
CalculationRuleMeaning.description: Name of the score calculation rule for questionnaires that are scored.

CheckBoxWithImageFlag.title: Checkbox with Image
CheckBoxWithImageFlag.description: Indicates whether you can add checkbox questions with image response type to the questionnaire. The default value is true.

CheckMultipleChoicesFlag.title: Check Multiple Choices
CheckMultipleChoicesFlag.description: Indicates whether you can add multiple choice checkbox response type questions to the questionnaire. The default value is true.

CreatedBy.title: Created By
CreatedBy.description: Name of the user who created the questionnaire template.

CreationDate.title: Creation Date
CreationDate.description: Date when the questionnaire template was created.

Editor.title: Owner
Editor.description: Person Identifier of the person having the privileges to edit the questionnaire template marked as private.

EditorPersonName.title: Person Name
EditorPersonName.description: Name of the person with privileges to edit the questionnaire template marked as private.

EditorPersonNumber.title: Person Number
EditorPersonNumber.description: Person number of the person with privileges to edit the questionnaire template that's marked private.

InUseFlag.title: In Use Code
InUseFlag.description: Indicates whether the questionnaire template is used by any questionnaires. The default value is false.

InUseMeaning.title: In Use
InUseMeaning.description: Meaning of the In Use Code attribute that Indicates whether the questionnaire template is used by any questionnaire.

InstructionText.title: Instruction Text
InstructionText.description: Instructions or additional information for the questionnaire template.

LastUpdateDate.title: Last Update Date
LastUpdateDate.description: Date when the questionnaire template was last updated.

LastUpdatedBy.title: Last Updated By
LastUpdatedBy.description: Name of the user who last updated the questionnaire template.

MaximumPossibleScore.title: Maximum Possible Score
MaximumPossibleScore.description: Sum of the maximum possible scores of all questions that are included in the questionnaire template.

MultipleChoicesListFlag.title: Multiple Choices from List
MultipleChoicesListFlag.description: Indicates whether questions with multiple choice list response type can be added to the questionnaire.

NoResponseFlag.title: No Response
NoResponseFlag.description: Indicates whether questions with no response type can be added to the questionnaire. The default value is true.

PrivacyFlag.title: Privacy Code
PrivacyFlag.description: Indicates whether the questionnaire template is private. If a template is marked private, only the person specified by the owner attribute can edit the template. The default value is false.

PrivacyMeaning.title: Privacy
PrivacyMeaning.description: Meaning of the privacy code indicating if the questionnaire template is marked private.

QuestionnaireTemplateId.title: Questionnaire Template Id
QuestionnaireTemplateId.description: Surrogate identifier for the questionnaire template.

RadioButtonListFlag.title: Radio Button List
RadioButtonListFlag.description: Indicates whether questions with single choice radio button response type can be added to the questionnaire. The default value is true.

RadioButtonWithImageFlag.title: Radio Button With Image
RadioButtonWithImageFlag.description: Indicates whether questions with radio button with image response type can be added to the questionnaire. The default value is true.

ScoreQuestionnaireFlag.title: Score Questionnaire
ScoreQuestionnaireFlag.description: Indicates whether the questionnaire is scored. The default value is false.

SectionOrderCode.title: Section Order Code
SectionOrderCode.description: Code that shows the ordering sequence of the questionnaire sections.

SectionOrderMeaning.title: Section Order
SectionOrderMeaning.description: Meaning of the section order code indicating the sequencing order of the questionnaire sections.

SectionPresentationCode.title: Section Presentation Code
SectionPresentationCode.description: Code that shows if the questionnaire is presented as stacked regions or without sections.

SectionPresentationMeaning.title: Section Presentation
SectionPresentationMeaning.description: Meaning of the section presentation code indicating the presentation of sections of the questionnaire.

SingleChoiceListFlag.title: Single Choice from List
SingleChoiceListFlag.description: Indicates whether questions with single choice list response type can be added to the questionnaire. The default value is true.

StatusMeaning.title: Status
StatusMeaning.description: Meaning of the status code for the questionnaire template.

SubscriberCode.title: Subscriber Code
SubscriberCode.description: Code for the subscriber using the questionnaire template. Valid values are defined in the list questionnaireSubscribersLOV.

SubscriberName.title: Subscriber Name
SubscriberName.description: Name of the subscriber using the questionnaire template.

VersionDescription.title: Version Description
VersionDescription.description: Description provided when a new version of a questionnaire template is created.

VersionNumber.title: Version Number
VersionNumber.description: Version number of the questionnaire template.

UploadedFileLength.description: The size of the attachment file.

UploadedFileName.title: Uploaded File Name
UploadedFileName.description: The name to assign to a new attachment file.

UploadedText.title: Uploaded Text
UploadedText.description: The text content for a new text attachment.

Uri.title: Uri

Url.title: Url

AllowAdditionalQuestionsFlag.title: Allow Additional Questions
AllowAdditionalQuestionsFlag.description: Indicates whether additional questions can be added to the questionnaire section when a response is requested. The default value is false.

CreatedBy.title: Created By
CreatedBy.description: Name of the user who created the section.

CreationDate.title: Creation Date
CreationDate.description: Date when the section was created.

IntroText.title: Introduction
IntroText.description: Introduction text for the questionnaire section.

LastUpdateDate.title: Last Update Date
LastUpdateDate.description: Date when the section was last updated.

LastUpdatedBy.title: Last Updated By
LastUpdatedBy.description: Name of the user who last updated the section.

MandatoryFlag.title: Required
MandatoryFlag.description: Indicates whether the questionnaire section is mandatory. The default value is false.

QuestionOrder.title: Question Order Code
QuestionOrder.description: Indicates the ordering sequence for the questions in a questionnaire section.

QuestionOrderMeaning.title: Question Order
QuestionOrderMeaning.description: Meaning of the question order code indicating the ordering sequence for the questions in a questionnaire section.

ResponseOrder.title: Response Order Code
ResponseOrder.description: Indicates the ordering sequence for the answer choices in questions included in a questionnaire section.

ResponseOrderMeaning.title: Response Order
ResponseOrderMeaning.description: Meaning of the response order code indicating the ordering sequence for the answer choices in questions.

SectionId.title: Section Id
SectionId.description: Surrogate identifier for the section in a questionnaire template.

SectionSequenceNumber.title: Section Sequence Number
SectionSequenceNumber.description: Sequence number for a section in a questionnaire template.

links.title: Links
links.description: The link relations associated with the resource instance.

questions.title: Questions
questions.description: The questions resource is a child of the questionnaireTemplates resource. It includes details of the questions included in a section of the questionnaire template.

AllowAdditionalCommentsFlag.title: Allow Additional Comments
AllowAdditionalCommentsFlag.description: Indicates whether a participant can provide additional comments while responding to a question. Valid values are Y and N. The default value is N.

ControllingQuestionCode.title: Question Code
ControllingQuestionCode.description: Question code for the controlling question that determines if the question is to be presented to the participant to respond. Valid values are defined in the list questionsLOV.

ControllingQuestionId.title: Controlling Question Id
ControllingQuestionId.description: Surrogate identifier for the controlling question.

CreatedBy.title: Created By
CreatedBy.description: Name of the user who added the question to the questionnaire template section.

CreationDate.title: Creation Date
CreationDate.description: Date when the question was added to the questionnaire template section.

InstructionsText.title: Instruction Text
InstructionsText.description: Instructions or additional information for the question.

KeepWithPreviousFlag.title: Keep with Previous
KeepWithPreviousFlag.description: Indicates whether the question is always paired with the previous question even when the question order is randomized. The default value is false.

LastUpdateDate.title: Last Update Date
LastUpdateDate.description: Date when the question was last updated in the questionnaire template section.

LastUpdatedBy.title: Last Updated By
LastUpdatedBy.description: Name of the user who last updated the question in the questionnaire template section.

LockQuestionFlag.title: Lock Question
LockQuestionFlag.description: Indicates whether the question can be removed or modified in the questionnaire. The default value is false.

MandatoryFlag.title: Mandatory
MandatoryFlag.description: Indicates whether the response to this question is mandatory. Participants can't skip this question without an answer. The default value is false.

MaximumPossibleScore.title: Maximum Possible Score
MaximumPossibleScore.description: Maximum possible score which is the sum of the maximum possible scores of the possible answer choices. This can be zero if none of the answer choices have a positive score defined.

QuestionCode.title: Question Code
QuestionCode.description: Question code for the question used in the questionnaire template section. Valid values are defined in the list questionsLOV.

QuestionType.title: Question Type Code
QuestionType.description: Question type code indicating the type of the question.

QuestionTypeMeaning.title: Question Type
QuestionTypeMeaning.description: Meaning of the question type code.

QuestionVersionNumber.title: Version Number
QuestionVersionNumber.description: Version number of the question used in the questionnaire template section.

QuestionnaireQuestionId.title: Questionnaire Question Id
QuestionnaireQuestionId.description: Surrogate identifier of the question in the questionnaire.

ResponseOrderCode.title: Response Order Code
ResponseOrderCode.description: Code that shows whether the question is enabled for random order. Possible values are Random to randomize the answer choices and Vertical to present the answer choices in the defined sequence.

ResponseOrderMeaning.title: Response Order
ResponseOrderMeaning.description: Meaning of the response order code.

ResponseType.title: Response Type Code
ResponseType.description: Response type for the question when presented to the participant. Valid values are defined in the questionnaireResponseTypesLOV.

ResponseTypeMeaning.title: Response Type
ResponseTypeMeaning.description: Meaning of the response type code.

SampleSize.title: Sample Size
SampleSize.description: Defines the number of questions presented to the participant of a questionnaire, when the question order is set to random.

SequenceNumber.title: Sequence Number
SequenceNumber.description: Sequence number of the question in a questionnaire template section.

answers.title: Answers
answers.description: The answers resource is a child of the questionnaireTemplates resource. It includes details of the answer choices defined for a question that's included in a questionnaire template section.

links.title: Links
links.description: The link relations associated with the resource instance.

QuestionAnswerId.title: Question Answer Id
QuestionAnswerId.description: Surrogate identifier for the answer choice defined for a question.

ResponseCode.title: Response Code
ResponseCode.description: Unique code for the answer choice defined for a question.

ResponseFeedback.title: Response Feedback
ResponseFeedback.description: Feedback provided to the participant when the answer choice is selected.

ShortDescription.title: Short Description
ShortDescription.description: Answer text for the answer choices of a question.

links.description: The link relations associated with the resource instance.

limit.description: The actual paging size used by the server.

links.description: The link relations associated with the resource instance.

totalResults.description: The estimated row count when "?totalResults=true", otherwise the count is not included.

Metadata-Context.description: If the REST API supports runtime customizations, the shape of the service may change during runtime. The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

REST-Framework-Version.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.
